## [00:00 - 01:19]

In my line of work, it's a very valuable skill to have to be able to communicate complex ideas. Do you have any recommendation on doing that? One of the most important skills you can learn is learning how to use analogies, metaphors, and similes. Analogies, metaphors, and similes reduce the complexity in any given communication. I'll give you a sample of that. When my clients used to want to work with me and I'd get the big corporates to go, Vin, we want you to train a thousand of our people, right? Now, can you tell us about what you teach? This is what I would say. I'll tell them, look, what we do is we dive into vocal mastery. That's critical because that's something known as a vocal image and visual image. Then we go into the complex different vocal archetypes to teach people when to use which archetype based on Bernice McCarthy's learning model. Then we'll also go into body language, because body language supports the thing you say. It's so complex in that there's an area known as the power sphere. My clients are like, what the frick is happening? Now, all I say is something very simple. On day one, what I'm going to do with the team is I'm going to teach them how to use their instrument. I'm going to teach them how to play their instrument in a way that's more engaging and in a way that's more memorable. We're going to teach them how to become more engaging, teach them how to play that instrument. Day two, we'll teach them how to write great music. That's storytelling. We're going to deep dive into how to use storytelling to be more influential. Now, my client's like, that's exactly what we need. Then if people want more complexity, I'll give it to them. That's a skill that adds more life, adds more engagement, and it makes things simpler.
